what_it_does: This file is a proposed design-substrate face for typography. It maps observed type measurements from nine source collections into structured additions and relational rules for dna/grammar.json and dna/tokens.json, including a canonical role table, a display-ramp extension, a computed content-budget rule, a figure-to-label scale-contrast rule, invariants, emphasis-register bindings, and a set of open questions and out-of-face deferrals.
observations: Proposes extending the existing display ramp with n=3, producing 60.75px for oversized hero figures.
Defines figure-to-label scale contrast as the signature ratio raised to k=2..3, yielding 2.25x..3.375x.
Introduces a computed content-budget rule: max_chars(role) ≈ zone_width(role) / char_advance(size, weight), with per-weight char advance.
Defines an eight-canonical-role resolver table that binds size, weight, colour, alignment reference, budget anchor, and one-line flag.
Appends invariants for heading-one-line, caption-may-wrap, and single-type-family.
Extends dna/tokens.json > type.weight with a display/extra-bold 800 anchor.
Reconciles source 'gold' section-head colours with the live ochre token, preserving a two-tone gold/ochre distinction.
Adds emphasis-register bindings: bold near-black for product names, gold for structure/inline figures, italic for coined terms and a once-per-page conviction headline.
States titles should be conversational claims, not topic labels, and notes deck-level title chaining.
Includes explicit provenance, status, conflict notes, and out-of-face deferrals for non-type findings.
standouts: The eight-role resolver table unifies roughly forty raw role measurements into one relational hierarchy.
The computed zone-width-relative content budget is treated as the central generative rule, with per-role char counts as observed anchors.
The figure-to-label contrast is derived from the same signature ratio that generates the type ramp.
The strict one-line invariant includes an explicit wrap carve-out for captions and balanced short labels.
concerns: Some values are inferred or estimated rather than measured, such as the regular-body char advance and the demotion of script_accent.
The n=3 hero tier versus the existing poster scale_tier remains an unresolved alternative.
The mono role is explicitly unsampled in this slice.
Several augmentations depend on 'Tim to confirm' decisions, making the file provisional.
The model-checker invariant for one-line headings depends on a rendering engine that is not specified here.
conventions: References existing alignment.by_role instead of redefining alignment coordinates.
Cites provenance and flags status (extends-existing / new) and conflict notes per augmentation.
Uses predicate/model checker language for invariants.
Breaks from the flat tokens.type.scale by adding a relational role layer in grammar.
notes: A dense, rule-driven proposal for turning a flat type scale into a relational, generative role system with computed budgets and invariants, plus a checklist of unresolved decisions.
questions: What is the exact source typeface family, given candidates like Poppins, Inter, Nunito, or Plus Jakarta Sans?
How does the generator evaluate the one-line model checker without a specified rendering engine?
What are the precise zone_width values per role in the source layouts?
Is the poster scale_tier already defined as a numeric ratio, or is it a separate concept from the display ramp?
How should the bold-vs-gold emphasis rule interact with existing colour tokens for inline links or other accent text?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is a structured interpretive ledger entry for a proposed type-design substrate, recording observations, rules, conflicts, and open questions.
apparent_themes: Relational/computed design tokens over fixed values.
Intentional content length and one-line discipline.
Separation of sampled anchors from derived rules.
intention_signals: It is trying to encode a generative typography system where any text role resolves to size, weight, colour, alignment, and budget by rule, so layouts can be generated or checked against a small set of invariants rather than manual specifications.
novelty: The computed content-budget rule using per-weight char advance and zone width; the figure-to-label contrast expressed as powers of the same signature ratio that generates the type ramp; the bold-vs-gold two-register vocabulary rule; and the conversational-claim title invariant with deck-level title chaining.
contribution: It contributes a canonical eight-role resolver table linking type, colour, alignment, and budget; a computed zone-width-relative content-budget formula with per-weight char advance; a scale-contrast rule tying figure-to-label size ratio to the signature ratio; invariants for one-line headings, caption wrap carve-out, single type family, and title-as-conversational-claim; an 800 weight anchor and hero display-ramp extension proposal; emphasis-register bindings; and a bounded set of open questions and out-of-face deferrals.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a proposed type-design substrate for the counterpart-design ledger that maps roughly forty observed type measurements from nine source collections into relational grammar rules, a canonical eight-role resolver table, computed content budgets, scale-contrast invariants, and emphasis registers for dna/grammar.json and dna/tokens.json.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to turn a flat sampled type scale into a generative, machine-checkable role system while surfacing unresolved decisions for the designer.
